2603.23308.title: Curriculum-Driven 3D CT Report Generation via Language-Free Visual Grafting and Zone-Constrained Compression
2603.23308.paper_summary: Automated radiology report generation from 3D computed tomography (CT) volumes is challenging due to extreme sequence lengths, severe class imbalance, and the tendency of large language models (LLMs) to ignore visual tokens in favor of linguistic priors. We present Ker-VLJEPA-3B, a four-phase curriculum learning framework for free-text report generation from thoracic CT volumes. A phased training curriculum progressively adapts a Llama 3.2 3B decoder to ground its output in visual features from a frozen, self-supervised encoder. Our visual backbone (LeJEPA ViT-Large) is trained via self-supervised joint-embedding prediction on unlabeled CTs, without text supervision. Unlike contrastive models (CLIP, BiomedCLIP), this language-free backbone yields modality-pure representations. Vision-language alignment is deferred to the curriculum's bridge and generation phases. This modality-agnostic design can integrate any self-supervised encoder into an LLM without paired text during foundation training. Methodological innovations include: (1) zone-constrained cross-attention compressing slice embeddings into 32 spatially-grounded visual tokens; (2) PCA whitening of anisotropic LLM embeddings; (3) a positive-findings-only strategy eliminating posterior collapse; (4) warm bridge initialization transferring projection weights; and (5) selective cross-attention freezing with elastic weight consolidation to prevent catastrophic forgetting. Evaluated on the CT-RATE benchmark (2,984 validation volumes, 18 classes), Ker-VLJEPA-3B achieves a macro F1 of 0.429, surpassing the state-of-the-art (U-VLM, macro F1 = 0.414) by 3.6%, and reaching 0.448 (+8.2%) with threshold optimization. Ablation studies confirm 56.6% of generation quality derives from patient-specific visual content. Code and weights are available.
2603.23308.paper_summary_zh: 自動生成3D計算機斷層掃描（CT）影像的放射學報告面臨挑戰，原因包括極端的序列長度、嚴重的類別不平衡，以及大型語言模型（LLMs）傾向於忽略視覺標記而偏向語言先驗。我們提出了Ker-VLJEPA-3B，一個四階段的課程學習框架，用於從胸部CT影像生成自由文本報告。分階段的訓練課程逐步調整Llama 3.2 3B解碼器，使其輸出基於來自凍結的自我監督編碼器的視覺特徵。我們的視覺骨幹（LeJEPA ViT-Large）通過對未標記CT進行自我監督的聯合嵌入預測進行訓練，無需文本監督。與對比模型（CLIP, BiomedCLIP）不同，這個無語言的骨幹產生純粹的模態表示。視覺-語言對齊被推遲到課程的橋接和生成階段。這種無模態的設計可以在基礎訓練期間將任何自我監督編碼器整合到LLM中，而無需配對文本。方法學創新包括：（1）區域約束的交叉注意力將切片嵌入壓縮為32個空間基礎的視覺標記；（2）對各向異性LLM嵌入進行PCA白化；（3）僅限正面發現的策略消除後驗崩潰；（4）溫暖橋接初始化轉移投影權重；以及（5）選擇性交叉注意力凍結與彈性權重整合以防止災難性遺忘。在CT-RATE基準（2,984個驗證影像，18個類別）上的評估中，Ker-VLJEPA-3B達到了0.429的宏F1，超越了最先進技術（U-VLM，宏F1 = 0.414）3.6%，並在閾值優化後達到0.448（+8.2%）。消融研究確認56.6%的生成質量源於患者特定的視覺內容。代碼和權重可用。
2603.23308.author: V. K. Cody Bumgardner et.al.
2603.23308.authors: V. K. Cody Bumgardner, Mitchell A. Klusty, Mahmut S. Gokmen, Evan W. Damron
